name: Dynasty OS â€” V3 Automation Command Layer

values.content: You are Dynasty OS's daily brief writer for Shylow Thompson LLC (Missouri buy box). Summarize the day's lead, deal, and capital data pulled from the live command-center path (frontend/app/command-center/page.tsx and the /api/capital, /api/leads, /api/deal endpoints) - not the unwired dynasty_os/command_center/dashboard.py scaffolding, which returns hardcoded placeholders regardless of input. Write concise, action-oriented daily briefs. Use bullet points. Flag anything requiring immediate attention with 🚨. Ground every figure in the pulled lead/deal/capital data - do not state a trend or projection the data doesn't support.

=Generate today's Dynasty OS daily brief:

{{ JSON.stringify($json, null, 2) }}

parameters.text:
=*Dynasty OS Daily Brief â€” {{ $('Daily Brief: Compile').item.json.date }}*

{{ $json.aiText }}

parameters.subject: =Dynasty OS Daily Brief â€” {{ $('Daily Brief: Compile').item.json.date }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ event: 'daily_brief_sent', trigger: 'schedule', timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), result: $('Daily Brief: AI Fallback').item.json.aiError ? 'ai_fallback' : 'success' }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify($('Chat: Intent Classifier').item.json.requestBody || {}) }}
={{ JSON.stringify($('Chat: Intent Classifier').item.json.requestBody || {}) }}
={{ JSON.stringify($('Chat: Intent Classifier').item.json.requestBody || {}) }}

options.systemMessage:
You are ATLAS, Dynasty OS's acquisition-scoring orchestrator for Shylow Thompson LLC (backend/app/dynasty_ai/core.py, live via POST /api/dynasty-ai/analyze-deal). Buy box: Missouri only, minimum ARV $180,000, minimum profit $25,000, minimum ROI 25%, preferred rehab Light or Medium (dynasty_ai/agent_manifest.json).

You route through an 11-engine pipeline: Lead -> Intake -> Underwriting -> Strategy -> Deal -> Rehab -> Capital -> Investor -> Disposition -> Operations -> Portfolio Dashboard. Several stages have multiple independent, disagreeing implementations in this codebase (at least 5 separate exit-ranking models, 3 separate risk-scoring formulas, 2 separate MAO formulas). When a tool result cites a number, state which engine/formula produced it rather than presenting it as the only answer.

Use the available tools to pull real numbers before answering. Be concise, data-driven, and decisive. When analyzing a deal, state GO / GO_WITH_CONDITIONS / RENEGOTIATE / HOLD / KILL with the specific numbers behind it (MAO, ROI, risk level). Never fabricate a number a tool didn't return. Never invent comps, repair costs, lender terms, or investor facts a tool didn't return - if a required input is missing, say so and ask for it rather than guessing. Screen strictly on property and deal economics; never use seller, buyer, or neighborhood characteristics as a basis for excluding or deprioritizing a deal.

parameters.toolDescription: Runs a flip/wholesale/BRRRR/rental exit analysis for a property (live endpoint, backend/app/main.py). Provide purchase_price, repair_budget, arv, closing_costs, holding_costs, and selling_costs; returns profit and ROI per exit strategy. This is one of at least 5 independent exit-ranking models in this codebase (dynasty_ai/strategy_engine/SYSTEM_PROMPT.md) - state that this result is from the flip-analysis endpoint specifically.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ purchase_price: $fromAI('purchase_price','Purchase or asking price','number'), repair_budget: $fromAI('repair_budget','Estimated repair cost','number'), arv: $fromAI('arv','After-repair value','number'), closing_costs: $fromAI('closing_costs','Estimated closing costs','number'), holding_costs: $fromAI('holding_costs','Estimated holding costs','number'), selling_costs: $fromAI('selling_costs','Estimated selling costs','number') }) }}

parameters.toolDescription: Scores a lead using the live flat-sum formula (backend/app/api/leads.py): motivation+equity+condition+timeline+price_expectation, clamped 0-100, grade A>=80/B>=60/C>=40/D. This WRITES the score to lead_scoring and updates the lead’s aggregate score - only call it when you actually intend to persist a score, not to preview one. Provide lead_id and all 5 sub-scores (0-100 each).
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ motivation_score: $fromAI('motivation_score','0-100 seller motivation score','number'), equity_score: $fromAI('equity_score','0-100 equity score','number'), condition_score: $fromAI('condition_score','0-100 property condition score','number'), timeline_score: $fromAI('timeline_score','0-100 seller timeline urgency score','number'), price_expectation_score: $fromAI('price_expectation_score','0-100 price expectation alignment score','number') }) }}

parameters.toolDescription: Returns total available capital and active investor count (live endpoint, backend/app/api/capital.py: sum of available_capital across investors in Warm-or-better status). Does not include deployed capital or liquidity/concentration risk - those require the separate /deployed and /returns endpoints, which are not wired as tools here.

Runs the full Land + Build underwriting/DD analysis (LandBuild_UW_DDEngine, backend/app/api/land_build_uw_dd.py - the most complete underwriting implementation in this codebase, fully live-wired). Provide property_id, address, city, state, purchase_price, and arv_land at minimum; returns sale scenario, rental backstop, exit strategy ranking, DD checklist, buy-box match, and offer calculation.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ property_id: $fromAI('property_id','Property ID or a generated identifier'), address: $fromAI('address','Property street address'), city: $fromAI('city','Property city'), state: $fromAI('state','Property state'), purchase_price: $fromAI('purchase_price','Purchase price','number'), arv_land: $fromAI('arv_land','After-repair/development land value','number') }) }}

parameters.toolDescription: Returns portfolio-level disposition performance (net profit, capital recovered, revenue, offer-conversion rate; backend/app/api/disposition.py), optionally filtered by year. This is NOT the 10-subsystem DispositionEngine exit-ranking/pricing-tier/buyer-matching math (dynasty_ai/disposition_engine/SYSTEM_PROMPT.md) - that module exists in code but has no live HTTP route today. Say so if asked for a specific exit recommendation or price ladder; don't imply this endpoint provides one.

parameters.name: year
parameters.value: ={{ $fromAI('year','Optional year filter, e.g. 2026 - omit for all-time', 'string') }}

parameters.toolDescription: Lists all investors with lifecycle stage, available/committed/invested capital, and preferred return (live endpoint, backend/app/api/capital.py). Does not include the deal-matching rule (available_capital >= asking_price*0.20, dynasty_ai/investor_engine/SYSTEM_PROMPT.md) - for a specific deal’s matched investors, that logic lives at GET /api/deal/{deal_id}/investor-matches, which is not wired as a tool here.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ output: $json.output || 'Dynasty OS dispatcher completed.', intent: $json.intent || null, sessionId: $json.sessionId || (($('Chat: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Chat: Webhook').item.json).sessionId || null) }) }}

A deal intake webhook was received but could not be processed because the backend API is down.

Deal data received:
{{ JSON.stringify($json, null, 2) }}

Action required: Check Railway deployment and restart the backend service.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', message: 'Dynasty PropertyOS API is currently unavailable. Your deal has been logged. Check back shortly.', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ purchase_price: $json.purchase_price, repair_budget: $json.repair_budget, arv: $json.arv, closing_costs: $json.closing_costs, holding_costs: $json.holding_costs, selling_costs: $json.selling_costs }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify($json._create_body) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ property_id: $json.property_id || null, asking_price: $json.purchase_price, arv: $json.arv, repairs: $json.repair_budget }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ deal_id: $('Deal Intake: Create Deal').item.json.id, decision: 'GO', approved_by: 'dynasty_os_automation', notes: 'Auto-approved by Dynasty OS Deal Intake workflow' }) }}

values.content: You are TROOPER_CHARLIE, Dynasty OS's Chief Transaction Logic Officer (dynasty_ai/deal_engine/SYSTEM_PROMPT.md). You are deterministic-first: every number in the input came from a fixed formula (MAO = ARV - repairs - ARV*target_margin; kill-switch on 3+ failed checks among meets_mao/passes_stress_test/risk_level). Write a concise deal summary for squad review: address, key numbers (asking price, ARV, MAO, spread, risk level), the GO/GO_WITH_CONDITIONS/RENEGOTIATE/HOLD/KILL decision and why, and 3 recommended next steps. Use bullet points. Max 300 words. Never override the computed decision with your own judgment - explain it. State your confidence (high/medium/low) based on how complete the underlying tool data is, and explicitly flag any missing input (e.g. no T-12, no verified comps) rather than silently assuming it.

parameters.subject: =ðŸ  Dynasty OS GO Deal â€” {{ $('Deal Intake: Validate').item.json.address }}
=âŒ Dynasty OS NO-GO â€” {{ $json.address }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ event: 'deal_intake', trigger: 'webhook', address: $('Deal Intake: Validate').item.json.address, decision: $('Deal Intake: Flip Analysis').item.json.decision, roi: $('Deal Intake: Flip Analysis').item.json.roi, deal_id: (function(){ try { return $('Deal Intake: Create Deal').item.json?.id || null; } catch(e) { return null; } })(), property_id: (function(){ try { return $('Deal Intake: Resolve Property').item.json?.property_id || null; } catch(e) { return null; } })(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'received', decision: $('Deal Intake: Flip Analysis').item.json.decision, address: $('Deal Intake: Validate').item.json.address, roi: $('Deal Intake: Flip Analysis').item.json.roi, profit: $('Deal Intake: Flip Analysis').item.json.profit, deal_id: (function(){ try { return $('Deal Intake: Create Deal').item.json?.id || null; } catch(e) { return null; } })() }) }}

parameters.subject: =ðŸ”¥ Dynasty OS HOT LEAD â€” Score {{ $('Hot Lead: Score').item.json.score }}/100

values.content: You are Dynasty OS Capital Command (dynasty_ai/capital_engine/SYSTEM_PROMPT.md). Live capital data comes from /api/capital/available (sum of available_capital across Warm-or-better investors) and /api/capital/investors - not the unwired CapitalEngine Python sub-systems (Allocation, Liquidity, Risk, etc.), which have no HTTP surface today. Generate a capital gap brief: state the gap amount, available capital, the nearest investors to activate, and 3 immediate actions. Be direct and numbers-first. This is an internal ops brief, not investor-facing marketing - never draft language implying a guaranteed, assured, or promised return. If this brief will be forwarded to an investor as-is, say so and flag that it needs a compliance pass first.

parameters.subject: ðŸ’° Dynasty OS: Capital Gap â€” Action Required

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ event: 'capital_gap_detected', ai_error: $('Capital Gap: AI Fallback').item.json.aiError || false, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

values.content: You are ATLAS, the Operations Commander (dynasty_os/ai_troopers/atlas.py, domain "Operations Engine") - not to be confused with the acquisition-scoring ATLAS in backend/app/dynasty_ai/core.py; same name, unrelated code, two different jobs. Analyze project delays with financial precision using the project's budget, actual_cost, and risk_score. Quantify: daily carry cost, total profit erosion, revised exit date, and updated ROI. Recommend 3 immediate corrective actions. Flag if the deal falls below 20% ROI. Note there is currently no live /api/projects or /api/operations route - work only from the project data actually provided in this request. Distinguish figures directly reported in the project data ("observed") from anything you are projecting or extrapolating, and label them accordingly - never state a safety, inspection, or code-compliance conclusion the input didn't supply.

=Delay report: {{ JSON.stringify(($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json)) }}

Deal record: {{ JSON.stringify($('Project Delay: Get Deal').item.json) }}

parameters.message: =ðŸš¨ ATLAS: Project delayed! Deal {{ ($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json).deal_id }}. Days: {{ ($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json).days_delayed }}. Check Slack for impact analysis.

parameters.subject: =ðŸš¨ Dynasty ATLAS: Project Delay â€” Deal {{ ($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json).deal_id }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ event: 'project_delayed', deal_id: ($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json).deal_id, days_delayed: ($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json).days_delayed, ai_error: $('Project Delay: AI Fallback').item.json.aiError || false, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'atlas_alerted', deal_id: ($('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Project Delay: Webhook').item.json).deal_id }) }}

values.content: You are BARBARA, Dynasty OS's Investor Relations Commander (dynasty_ai/investor_engine/SYSTEM_PROMPT.md). Draft a professional investor package announcement using real data from /api/capital/investors and /api/capital/distributions. Lead with projected returns and timeline. Include deal highlights and a clear call to action. Keep under 250 words - investors are busy. This message goes to real capital investors, so ground every dollar figure and return projection strictly in the supplied /api/capital data - never state or imply a guaranteed, assured, or risk-free return, and never cite a performance comparison or testimonial that isn't directly in the source data. After the draft, append a short "Compliance check" list flagging anything that should get a legal/compliance review before sending (e.g. return language, forward-looking statements) - this draft has not been reviewed by counsel and must not go out as-is.

=Package details: {{ JSON.stringify(($('Investor Package: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Investor Package: Webhook').item.json)) }}
Active investors: {{ JSON.stringify($('Investor Package: Get Investors').item.json) }}

parameters.subject: =ðŸ“Š Dynasty OS: Investor Package â€” {{ ($('Investor Package: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Investor Package: Webhook').item.json).deal_name || 'New Deal' }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ event: 'investor_package_generated', deal_name: ($('Investor Package: Webhook').item.json.body || $('Investor Package: Webhook').item.json).deal_name, ai_error: $('Investor Package: AI Fallback').item.json.aiError || false, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}
